content:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: Acknowledged: will change idcg to sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)), removing the min()/ideal_length clamp against len(retrieved_ids), and will preserve this bound when threading relevance_scores through the idcg sum.
note: Implementing both changes now in metrics.py

content:
type: DONE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Implemented both changes in NDCG.compute(): your idcg fix (now sums over range(1, len(expected_ids)+1)) plus my relevance_scores param. Heads up: this changes semantics for existing tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py::test_ndcg 'Case 4: More relevant docs than retrieved' (expected 1.0, now scores ~0.637 since idcg is no longer capped by len(retrieved_ids)). I'm leaving test files untouched per my task constraints — you may want to update that test case in your own patch since it encodes the old idcg behavior your fix intentionally changes.
